name: Manila Clams with Black Bean and Ginger Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 dozen manila clams or new zealand cockles
1/4 cup shao hsing or dry sherry
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the peanut oil in a wok over medium-high heat.
2. Stir-fry the ginger, garlic, jalapeno, and scallions for 1 minute.
3. Add the clams to the wok and stir well to combine with the aromatics.
4. Pour in the shao hsing (dry sherry) and a scant 3/4 cup of water.
5. Cover the wok and bring to a boil. Cook until the clams open, about 2 to 3 minutes.
6. Carefully remove the clams with tongs and place them on a plate; let any liquid drain back into the wok.
7. In the same wok, stir together the oyster sauce, soy sauce, sugar, and crushed black beans.
8. Cook over medium-high heat for 30 seconds.
9. Mix cornstarch with a little water in a small bowl to form a milky slurry; add this mixture to the hot liquid in the wok, stirring continuously until it thickens.
10. Stir in the sesame oil and return the clams to the wok.
11. Toss the clams to coat them evenly and serve immediately.
Yield: 4 servings as a first course
